name: Cafcass AI Scribe
description:
Led delivery of Scribe, a GenAI letter-generation platform for children’s social workers, from PoC to production. Scale: 1,500+ advisors, 80,000+ letters/month.

  • Multi-modal output (text and audio) for neurodiverse children and English-language barriers.
  • Evaluation frameworks: cosine similarity, ROUGE, Flesch-Kincaid, SMOG.
  • Responsible AI: access controls, audit trails, privacy-by-design, UK data residency.
  • Gartner Eye on Innovation runner-up; Digital Leaders ‘AI for Ethics, Trust and Governance’ shortlist.

name: .NET Legacy Modernisation Workshop
description: Authored a complete .NET legacy modernisation workshop end-to-end with Claude Code; delivered to senior .NET engineers at UKHSA. Demonstrates how AI coding assistants accelerate modernisation of legacy codebases to modern .NET.

name: Claude & Copilot Custom Instructions Workshop
description: A practical workshop covering custom instructions for both Claude and GitHub Copilot. Helps engineering teams configure AI coding assistants to follow team conventions, coding standards, and project-specific patterns, maximising productivity and consistency.

name: Knowledge Discovery Portal (Wavemaker)
description:
Connected over 8,000 global employees by delivering a knowledge-discovery portal with semantic search, relevance scoring, and Microsoft Teams integration. Powered by a RAG-style pipeline using Azure QnA Maker and LUIS.

  • Single-access repository portal with knowledge search and chatbot functions.
  • Teams integration for seamless workplace access.

name: Online Application Form
description: The development of an Online Application Form enabled the company to collect & check a wide range of complete information set from each applicant on a timely manner, required by industry specific British Standards. A plugged in Uniform Order Application resulted in reduced mobilisation timeframes by 2 days in average, which is considered a valuable time saving in the manned guarding sector. Both of these contributed to the company’s paperless office objective.

name: Project Neo
description: It's never easy to paint a complete picture about all available information on a transparent and easily understandable way. However, Smiths News' internal platform is able to do that. Considering all deliverable newspapers and packages, store locations, and missing items, as well as returns, automatically generated best routes are presented to warehouse managers to help them re-arrange deliveries for most efficient delivery routes. The portal, used by all 39 distribution centres within Smiths News on a daily basis, has also increased the level of accountability in certain positions thanks to user action tracking, and higher level of transparency.

name: Employee Platform
description: The purpose of the Employee Platform was to create a virtual company culture, to be developed into a social network & marketing tool, where current workforce can connect to potential interns as well as former employees. The platform contains user profiles, events & holiday planner, reporting facility, automatic notifications, archiving options, and mapping technology. Beyond the transparency it provides, control is given to HR managers, and employees at all levels benefit from reduced communication response times.

name: Project Eloquent
description:
In order to improve my development skills using MEAN stack, as a personal project, I’ve built a website for the apartments owned by my family in Dubrovnik. I’ve used Amazon Web Services as hosting provider, which gave me a great opportunity to experiment with different kinds of services included in AWS.

On the website one can find the apartments’ details, including a map with their locations, a gallery, a list of utilities provided, the distances between points of interest (like restaurant, supermarket, ATM, gas station, airport, etc.), as well as lots of useful information & interesting facts about Dubrovnik, its surroundings, and Croatia in general. Some advanced features also got included, like Google charts featuring average temperature & precipitation graphs, and a currency calculator, which retrieves the daily course list and converts the top 3 currencies (GBP, USD, EUR) into the local currency (KN - Kuna). On top of that I’ve also built a booking facility, however this has been requested to be removed by my uncle, who prefers to handle bookings on a different way.

name: Pass My Parcel Tracking
description: The purpose of a parcel delivery tracking platform for Pass My Parcel was to change the way information is viewed, enabling clients, depot supervisors and the support team, to see the full log of package delivery stages including their status, as well as location and timings. Automatically scheduled reminders and updates are to make sure that clients are more informed. Being able to see every step of the delivery process with in-depth information can truly empower the customer support team, while depot supervisors can make better plans in regards to stock management and delivery planning.

name: SNapp
description: With no budgets for further third-party development, the Smiths News App had to be developed in-house. Having a search facility for different attributes of a product was a long requested improvement by many app users. Allowing for locking orders for upcoming deliveries saves time for the users when ordering. These, along with various bug fixes, has improved not just the UI, but the overall user experience, resulting in increased customer satisfaction.

name: Portfolio Migration
description:
I’ve migrated my old portfolio hosted by a local hosting provider in Croatia to Amazon Web Services.

Since AWS allows me to utilise new technologies and modern approaches, I consider this an ongoing project of mine. I wish to do application containerazation, and play around with automated deployment, as well as availability zones and load balancing.
